item.title: Malaysia Is Running Out Of Caregivers And Time — Rashidi Yahaya
item.description:
Source: CodeBlue (Malaysia Health)
Malaysia requires a long-term care insurance framework that spreads risk across the population and creates a sustainable financing pool, built before the demographic wave hits full force, not after. The post Malaysia Is Running Out Of Caregivers And Time — Rashidi Yahaya appeared first on CodeBlue .
Matched: breaking(+3), urgent(+3), policy(+3), budget(+3), law(+3), parliament(+2), ministry(+2), war(+3), economy(+2), ai(+3), who(+2)

item.title: LIAM Alleges Average Medical Insurance Claim Only RM12,000
item.description:
Source: CodeBlue (Malaysia Health)
In defending the RM100k annual limit under the Base MHIT plan, LIAM claims that the average medical claim is only RM12,000, even though many ITOs in the insurance industry regularly tout policies with RM1m or higher annual limits for medical emergencies. The post LIAM Alleges Average Medical Insurance Claim Only RM12,000 appeared first on CodeBlue .
Matched: policy(+3), war(+3), ai(+3), dengue(+3)

item.title: Health PSSC Moots Expansion Of Insurance Coverage For Mental Health Services
item.description:
Source: CodeBlue (Malaysia Health)
The Health PSSC recommends expanding insurance coverage for mental health services, warning that limited coverage is pushing patients to pay out of pocket and straining public hospitals. Preventive care remains largely excluded from insurance. The post Health PSSC Moots Expansion Of Insurance Coverage For Mental Health Services appeared first on CodeBlue .
Matched: parliament(+2), ministry(+2), war(+3), ai(+3), who(+2)

item.title: Expert: Gradually Replace PTDs In MOH With Public Health, Allied Health Professionals
item.description:
Source: CodeBlue (Malaysia Health)
A public health medicine specialist (PHMS) wants a study to identify MOH's admin posts held by PTDs that can be gradually filled by PHMS or allied health professionals trained in health system management. PTDs are still relevant in some areas in HQ/state. The post Expert: Gradually Replace PTDs In MOH With Public Health, Allied Health Professionals appeared first on CodeBlue .
Matched: policy(+3), ministry(+2), ai(+3), who(+2)

item.title: When Bariatric Surgery is Dismissed As ‘Cosmetic’: What We Are Missing
item.description:
Source: CodeBlue (Malaysia Health)
Bariatric surgery is often dismissed as “cosmetic”, but it is a medical intervention for severe obesity and metabolic disease. It drives significant weight loss, reduces metabolic comorbidities like diabetes and hypertension, and improves quality of life. The post When Bariatric Surgery is Dismissed As ‘Cosmetic’: What We Are Missing appeared first on CodeBlue .
Matched: policy(+3), ai(+3), who(+2), diabetes(+2)

item.title: A bold new plan could finally cure type 1 diabetes
item.description:
Source: ScienceDaily Health (Health)
Researchers are developing a two-part therapy for type 1 diabetes: lab-made insulin-producing cells paired with custom-engineered immune cells that protect them. The goal is to stop the immune system from destroying transplanted cells — without using immunosuppressive drugs. Backed by $1 million in funding, the team hopes to create a ready-to-use treatment that could work even for people who have had diabetes for years. The approach could transform how the disease is treated.
Matched: ai(+3), funding(+2), who(+2), diabetes(+2)

item.title: Over Half Of Hospital Patients At Risk Of Disease-Related Malnutrition, Say Dietitians
item.description:
Source: CodeBlue (Malaysia Health)
The Malaysian Dietitians’ Association says over half of hospital patients – and up to 70% among cancer patients – are at risk of disease-related malnutrition, doubling readmission risk and raising costs by 20-30%. Medical nutrition therapy is not covered by insurance. The post Over Half Of Hospital Patients At Risk Of Disease-Related Malnutrition, Say Dietitians appeared first on CodeBlue .
Matched: parliament(+2), ai(+3), who(+2), cancer(+2)

item.title: Florida restricts access to HIV drugs for thousands
item.description:
Source: Healio News (Health)
The Florida Department of Health imposed an emergency rule on Sunday that could prevent thousands of lower-income or uninsured people from being able to pay for their HIV medication. The rule drastically cut funding and eligibility for the state’s AIDS Drug Assistance Program (ADAP), impacting an estimated 16,000 residents, according to the HIV Medicine Association. The cuts were driven by a reported $120 million budget deficit, rising insurance premiums and declining federal assistance. Previously, eligibility for the program was capped at 400% of the federal poverty level — which is under
Matched: budget(+3), ai(+3), funding(+2)

item.title: EVP Beats Cisplatin for Resectable MIBC
item.description:
Source: Medscape (Health)
As the first chemotherapy-free regimen to beat gemcitabine/cisplatin, perioperative enfortumab vedotin plus pembrolizumab could shift muscle-invasive bladder cancer care — but questions remain. Medscape Medical News
Matched: war(+3), ai(+3), cancer(+2)

item.title: Public Sector Short Of Over 13,000 Specialists To Meet 2030 Needs: MOH
item.description:
Source: CodeBlue (Malaysia Health)
According to MOH data, Malaysia’s public health care sector needs 22,435 specialist doctors by 2030, compared to 9,040 currently in service – a shortfall of 13,395. At current output, only 4,000 to 5,600 specialists would be produced by 2030, less than half the gap. The post Public Sector Short Of Over 13,000 Specialists To Meet 2030 Needs: MOH appeared first on CodeBlue .
Matched: ministry(+2), war(+3), ai(+3)

item.title: Alopecia Areata May Raise Risk for Autoimmune Diseases
item.description:
Source: Medscape (Health)
Patients with new-onset alopecia areata are at increased 10-year risk of developing several immune-mediated inflammatory diseases, according to a study. Medscape Medical News
Matched: war(+3), ai(+3)

item.title: Changes in Body Image and Pain Tied to Depression in Cushing
item.description:
Source: Medscape (Health)
A recent longitudinal cohort study shows a statistically significant relationship between body image disturbance, pain, and persistent depression in patients with Cushing syndrome. Medscape News UK
Matched: war(+3), ai(+3)

item.title: CKM Syndrome Recognition and Collaboration Gaining Traction
item.description:
Source: Medscape (Health)
While first-ever guidelines are in the works, experts across specialties share their take on the current state of preventing, diagnosing, and managing CKM syndrome. Medscape Medical News
Matched: war(+3), ai(+3)

item.title: Oral insulin fails to slow type 1 diabetes progression
item.description:
Source: Healio News (Health)
High-dose oral insulin did not reduce risk for progression to type 1 diabetes among children with stage 1 disease, according to data from a phase 2 trial published in Diabetes Care. In the Fr1da Insulin Intervention trial conducted in Germany, 220 children aged 2 to 12 years with stage 1 type 1 diabetes were randomly assigned to once-daily oral insulin (Eli Lilly) or placebo for 1 year (50.9% girls; median age, 4.8 years). Children were considered to have stage 1 type 1 diabetes if they tested positive for more than one type 1 diabetes autoantibody and had normal glucose levels at baseline.
Matched: ai(+3), diabetes(+2)

item.title: Persistent smokers face lower Parkinson’s risks vs. quitters
item.description:
Source: Healio News (Health)
Adults who quit smoking had a heightened risk for Parkinson’s disease vs. those who continued to smoke, according to results published in Neurology. However, those reporting quitting had a reduced risk for all-cause mortality vs. those who continued smoking, according to researchers. “Our study suggests a lower risk of Parkinson’s is not necessarily associated with how long someone smokes, but rather if they are currently smoking,” Jun-Hyuk Lee, MD, PhD, assistant professor at Eulji University School of Medicine in South Korea, said in a press release related to the study. Using data from the
Matched: ai(+3), who(+2)

item.title: IHH Healthcare Malaysia Marks Third Consecutive Year in Newsweek’s World’s Best Hospitals 2026 Rankings
item.description:
Source: CodeBlue (Malaysia Health)
The continued recognition reflects governance systems that are firmly established across its hospitals, with patient safety and care standards applied consistently across specialties and services. The post IHH Healthcare Malaysia Marks Third Consecutive Year in Newsweek’s World’s Best Hospitals 2026 Rankings appeared first on CodeBlue .
Matched: court(+2), ai(+3)

item.title: How healthcare organizations can build ransomware resilience
item.description:
Source: MobiHealthNews (Health)
Scott Doerr, virtual CISO (vCISO) at Fortified Health Security, previews his upcoming talk at the 2026 HIMSS Global Health Conference & Exposition in March, where he will discuss how healthcare companies can strengthen their preparedness for ransomware attacks. MobiHealthNews: You will be discussing ransomware attacks against healthcare organizations. Can you give us a bit more detail about your talk?
Matched: war(+3), ai(+3)

item.title: NHS Boosts Bone Scanning With 20 New DEXA Units
item.description:
Source: Medscape (Health)
Twenty new DEXA scanners will expand NHS capacity in England, aiming to speed diagnosis and reduce fragility fractures. Medscape News UK
Matched: war(+3), ai(+3)

item.title: Virtual reality takes next step in eye care
item.description:
Source: Healio News (Health)
Click here to read the At Issue to this Cover Story. In the last 2 years, virtual reality and augmented reality have taken a step toward more practical applications in eye care. In 2024, Healio | OSN looked at this digital technology as it was making its way into physicians’ and patients’ hands. Since then, companies have introduced devices and programs aimed at improving educational and medical experiences for surgeons and patients alike. IOL simulators such as Immersive IOL Simulator (VirtuaLens) and InSightVR (Greenman) are designed to give patients a preview of their vision after IOL
Matched: war(+3), ai(+3)

item.title: Space Nursing: A New Frontier In Health Care — Mohd Irwan Hakeem Mohd Arif
item.description:
Source: CodeBlue (Malaysia Health)
Nursing is more than bedside care; it is scientific, research-driven, and adaptive. Space nursing demonstrates how far nurses can extend their expertise, reaching environments once thought unreachable. The post Space Nursing: A New Frontier In Health Care — Mohd Irwan Hakeem Mohd Arif appeared first on CodeBlue .
Matched: war(+3), ai(+3)
